Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix multi-user 'interaction failed' with defer + per-session lock

All ScoreboardView and FoulSelectView button/select callbacks now:
1. Call interaction.response.defer() immediately to acknowledge Discord
   within the 3-second window regardless of processing time
2. Acquire the per-session asyncio.Lock (added to SnookerSession._lock)
   to serialize concurrent state mutations from multiple users
3. Call interaction.edit_original_response() after the lock is released

This eliminates race conditions and timeout failures when two users
operate the bot simultaneously from separate devices.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(daemon): stop ignoring cancel/load results, add global error middleware, harden Timeouts

- Part 1: Cancel and load-script endpoints now check result strings for
  errors and return 500 with error details instead of always reporting
  success. Follows existing hardReset pattern.

- Part 2: Add errorHandlingMiddleware as ASP.NET pipeline middleware so
  ALL routes get exception handling automatically. Previously 5 of 32
  endpoints lacked withErrorHandling wrappers. Remove per-endpoint
  withErrorHandling calls since middleware now covers everything. SSE
  streams are handled safely via Response.HasStarted check.

- Part 3: Add ValidTimeout single-case DU with create/value for
  validated timeout ranges (1s-10min). Make mutable test timeouts
  thread-safe with locks. setPerTestTimeout and setGlobalTestRunTimeout
  now validate via ValidTimeout before accepting.

- Part 4: Add env var overrides for top 5 timeouts:
  SAGEFS_WARMUP_INACTIVITY_SECONDS, SAGEFS_PER_TEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
  SAGEFS_BUILD_TIMEOUT_MINUTES, SAGEFS_WARMUP_MAX_MINUTES,
  SAGEFS_WORKER_HTTP_READ_SECONDS. Read once at module init with
  envOrDefault/envOrDefaultMinutes helpers.

- Part 5: Add TimeoutsTests covering ValidTimeout validation, thread-safe
  setter rejection/acceptance, env var override positivity checks, and
  static timeout value assertions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix DblclickZoomInteraction not disabling double click zoom (#113) (#120)

.NET's camelCase JSON serializer converts DblclickZoomInteraction to
dblclickZoomInteraction (lowercase 'c' in click), but the Azure Maps SDK
expects dblClickZoomInteraction (capital 'C' in Click). The property was
silently ignored, so double click zoom was never disabled.

Fix by adding [JsonPropertyName("dblClickZoomInteraction")] to ensure
the correct key is sent to the SDK.

Fixes #113

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth -> clone-deep -> is-plain-object@5 is
ESM-only and crashes in Vercel's CJS runtime. On Vercel, use plain
playwright-core directly (no addExtra/stealth) to avoid the broken
dep chain. Stealth plugin still used locally.

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

that mean for out project?


</details>

— coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for

commit.message:
Update R/update_readme_log.R

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Multi-layer model with spectral norm clamping

- model.py: Support num_layers distinct W matrices, applied sequentially.
  Orthogonal init (spectral norm 1.0), ReLU provides natural contraction.
- train.py: Spectral norm clamping after each optimizer step prevents
  instability. Added --num-layers and --device args.
- generate.py: NPU path makes num_layers calls per character step,
  swapping W between calls. Added --depth override for inference.
- README.md: Added Phase 4 char LM section with usage and results.

8-layer model (148K params) reaches val loss 3.17 vs 1-layer 3.47.
NPU throughput: 287K-342K total chars/s (1152 parallel sequences).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update R/update_readme_log.R

Filter out conflicts where both PRs have the same author

If a developer has two of their own PRs conflicting, they likely
already know about it and don't need a Slack notification.

Changes:
- Added same-author filtering after conflict detection
- Filters out conflicts where pr_a.author == pr_b.author
- Prints count of filtered conflicts when > 0
- Added test to verify filtering behavior

Example output:

Track and display set duration in full mode

- SetState.started_at records when the set begins
- save_current_set() computes duration_secs at save time
- DB: duration_secs INTEGER column added (CREATE + ALTER IF NOT EXISTS)
- save_set() persists it; get_completed_sessions() surfaces it
- Scoreboard embed shows duration in last completed set results field
- /history shows duration inline with each set's score line

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(scrape): add Playwright fallback chain (BrightData -> Playwright -> plain)

Each article now tries three strategies in order:
1. BrightData Web Unlocker (if BRIGHTDATA_API_KEY set)
2. Playwright headless Chromium (if installed)
3. Plain requests (last resort)

A single browser context is reused across all articles for efficiency.
Playwright is imported lazily so the script degrades gracefully if not installed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: only upload task-created files to gist, ignore project files

Filter by birthtime instead of mtime, exclude known project files
(package.json, config, README, etc.) to prevent uploading unrelated
files as task output.

Feature/dsl (#2)

* feat: agent self-registration with runner capabilities

- Add RegisterRequest, RegisterResponse, HeartbeatRequest to shared types
- Add runner capability fields to agent Config (name, tags, cpu, mem, gpu, arch)
- Add register() and heartbeat() to agent ServerClient
- Agent registers on startup, sends heartbeat every 30s
- Add POST /api/agent/register and /api/agent/heartbeat server endpoints
- Add runners.rs DB module with register_runner and heartbeat_runner
- Update claim_job to accept and store optional runner_id
- Consolidate runner migration with unique name index for upsert

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: implement Forgefile DSL with per-job runner selection

Introduce a custom DSL (Forgefile) to replace foundry.toml for pipeline
definitions. This is a ground-up implementation including:

Parser & Language:
- Custom lexer (logos-based) with keywords, string interpolation, durations
- Recursive descent parser producing a typed AST
- Semantic validator (unique names, DAG cycle detection, reference checks)
- 75 tests across unit, integration, and end-to-end suites

Runner Selection:
- Agents self-register with capabilities (tags, cpu, mem, gpu, arch)
- Named runner definitions in Forgefile (runner "fast" { ... })
- Server-side matching engine routes jobs to capable runners
- DB migration for runner table and job runner requirements

DSL Features:
- Trigger blocks: on push(), pr(), schedule()
- Stage dependencies via needs (DAG)
- Secrets from Vault with key aliasing
- Service container declarations
- Matrix build definitions
- Deploy blocks with domain/compose support
- Duration literals (10m, 1h), multiline strings, conditions

Integration:
- Agent parses Forgefile from repo, converts to execution plan
- Vault secrets wired with alias support
- Replaces foundry.toml (removed from repo)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: allow keywords as identifiers in name positions

Add Token::as_ident() method that converts any keyword token to its
string representation. Update Parser::expect_ident() to use it, so
keywords like 'deploy', 'image', 'port' etc. are accepted wherever
an identifier is expected (stage names, service names, runner refs,
needs refs, secret keys, etc.).

Remove the workaround in test_repo_forgefile_parses that swallowed
parse errors as a known limitation. Add test_keyword_as_stage_name
to verify 'stage deploy { ... }' parses correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove unused forgefile dependency and simplify allow_failure syntax in Forgefile

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove app registration requirement

Use Microsoft Graph PowerShell well-known client ID (14d82eec...)
instead of requiring users to register their own Entra ID app.
This is a first-party Microsoft app pre-registered in every tenant.

- clientId is now optional in config (defaults to Graph PowerShell)
- Removed Entra ID app registration section from README
- Tested device-code flow with all required scopes

commit.message:
fix: load products from API (not static JSON) + fix scraper on Vercel

- catalog/page.tsx, page.tsx, new-arrivals/page.tsx: replace static
  products.json import with useEffect fetch('/api/products') so products
  come from Supabase on Vercel instead of an empty local file
- Add skeleton loading states on all three pages
- fetch-meesho: detect Akamai challenge page (200 OK but blocked HTML)
  and return null so fallback is triggered correctly
- fetch-meesho: on Vercel (process.env.VERCEL), skip Playwright entirely
  and return 503 immediately to show bookmarklet fallback UI

commit.message:
fix: set CaskaydiaCove NF font in WT defaults so OMP glyphs render

Without this, oh-my-posh installs the Nerd Font but Windows Terminal
keeps using the default Cascadia Code (no glyphs) - all OMP symbols
render as diamond fallback characters.

Sets profiles.defaults.font.face = 'CaskaydiaCove NF' in WT settings.json
during the Section 11 Windows Terminal configuration step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Sprint 57: StatusBar centering, SplitPane.dragInBounds, FocusModel<'id>, Focus.style, VirtualTable.filter, ModalStack

- Fix StatusBar.view centering: 3-child row -> 5-child row with two Fill spacers
- Add SplitPane.handleDragInBounds + dragSubInBounds (pane-relative coords)
- Add FocusModel<'id> type + module (ID-based focus, survives reordering)
- Add Focus.style and Focus.defaultStyle (styled focus highlight helpers)
- Add Filter field to VirtualTableConfig + setFilter/clearFilter/displayItems
- Add ModalLayer + ModalStack + module ModalStack (layered overlay manager)
- 48 new TDD tests, 2346 total passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Bash script that reads tool_input JSON from stdin, maps edited file paths
to affected audit dimensions, and merges stale dims into .solution-audit-latest.json.

  bash while-read IFS to avoid colon-in-URL splitting on https:// paths
- check-examples.sh: count all .md files walked (not just those with validated
  blocks) so files_checked reflects true population; wrap os.walk loop in
  try/except to emit __STATS__:ERROR:0 and exit 1 on unhandled crash; remove
  unused tempfile import

commit.message:
CODER: 19.1 — Decompose event/mod.rs into navigation, actions, search, keys sub-modules

Split 520-line event/mod.rs into 5 focused files:
- navigation.rs (73 lines): split adjust + 6 navigate_* helpers
- actions.rs (158 lines): 15 action functions + platform utilities
- search.rs (47 lines): search input + apply_search
- keys.rs (219 lines): 7 key handler functions
- mod.rs (49 lines): thin dispatcher with handle_key_event

Updated 5 test files to reference new sub-module paths for

All 2094+ tests pass, zero clippy warnings.
